alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google Devices and Services in strategy and operations roles with experience scaling global commercial operations and transformation programs; product ownership including requirements definition, logic design, and UAT.
alignments.job_requirement: Lead the technical development of system architectures and define requirements for Safety Critical Systems

alignments.candidate_evidence: Scaling global commercial operations indicates broad stakeholder management; transformation program leadership implies executive-level communication and reporting.
alignments.job_requirement: Work closely with internal and external stakeholders; provide regular reporting to Chief Engineering and Programme Management

alignments.candidate_evidence: Positioning as Strategy and Operations leader with eight years at Google, including scaling programs and transformation work; professional English fluency.
alignments.job_requirement: Possess excellent communication skills and the ability to report and present results clearly and effectively to both internal and external stakeholders

gaps.gap: No aerospace engineering background; Bachelor's or Master's in Aerospace Engineering required
gaps.mitigation: Candidate would need to undertake formal aerospace engineering education or intensive crash course; unlikely to close quickly without degree-level study.

gaps.gap: No hands-on avionics hardware/software development experience; JD requires 'led the design and development of safety critical hardware and software for an airborne platform'
gaps.mitigation: Would require months to years of direct avionics systems engineering experience in a safety-critical airborne context; cannot be mitigated through operations or strategy background alone.

gaps.gap: No demonstrated knowledge of ARP4754, ARP4761, DO-178C, DO-254 aviation standards; these are explicitly required
gaps.mitigation: Certification standards are taught in formal avionics and aerospace engineering programs or through specialized industry training; does not map to operational or strategy domain knowledge.

gaps.gap: No stated background in Guidance Navigation & Control or air vehicle management systems; role requires 'deep knowledge'
gaps.mitigation: These are specialized aerospace subsystems requiring formal engineering education and domain-specific project experience.

gaps.gap: No established relationship with civil or military aviation authority; JD explicitly requires this
gaps.mitigation: Could potentially be developed through engagement in a new role, but candidate has no aviation regulatory experience to date.

hard_blockers.evidence: Hold a relevant degree such as a Bachelor's or Master's in Aerospace Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field; Have led the design and development of safety critical hardware and software for an airborne platform including tasks related to systems engineering, safety, aircraft certification, and avionics integration; Have a deep knowledge of the development of avionics complex HW & SW development and have experience in the application of system level (ARP4754 and ARP4761) and SW/HW level (DO-178C and DO-254) standards to safety critical systems. Candidate profile shows no aerospace engineering degree, no avionics or airborne hardware/software development experience, and no stated knowledge of aviation certification standards.

output.level_rationale: Role requires led design and development of safety-critical airborne systems, planning and leading systems engineering lifecycle, reporting to Chief Engineering and Programme Management, plus deep domain expertise in avionics, GN&C, and aviation standards. These signal established senior individual-contributor or lead responsibility in a specialized, high-stakes domain. Estimated L5 based on ownership scope and technical leadership accountability, though company stage (stated as unknown unless in JD) lowers confidence slightly.
output.summary: This is a specialist safety-critical avionics systems engineering role requiring aerospace engineering degree, led airborne HW/SW development, and deep knowledge of aviation standards (ARP4754, ARP4761, DO-178C, DO-254). The candidate is a Strategy and Operations leader with eight years at Google—strong in scaling operations, transformation, and stakeholder management, but with no aerospace engineering education, no avionics or airborne hardware/software development background, and no aviation regulatory experience. The role family (deep technical engineering in a safety-critical domain) is orthogonal to the candidate's strategic operations positioning. Fundamental domain expertise and engineering credentials are missing and cannot be rapidly bridged through operations or strategy experience alone.
uncertainties: Candidate's specific product engineering work (requirements, UAT, rollout) at Google Devices is not detailed; unclear whether any of this touched hardware safety criticality or regulatory compliance domains.
Helsing company stage and team structure not stated in JD; level estimate assumes established engineering governance, but early-stage context could shift scope.
No information on whether candidate has pursued any aerospace or formal systems engineering study post-Google.
